title: A Fresh Start for Your Career in Recruitment Consulting
description: * **Channel your drive into a role that fuels a company in rapid expansion.** * **Your effort sets the pace – fast and completely performance\-based career progression.** * **Join a business that rewards ambition and invests in your future.** What if your career moved forward exactly as fast as you’re willing to push it? What if you didn’t have to wait for someone else’s approval to take the next step? At **Access** , your results speak louder than anything else. Here, **progression isn’t about time served, it’s about performance** . If you bring energy, resilience, and hunger to succeed, there’s no ceiling on what you can achieve. Maybe you’re already in recruitment and ready for a step up. Or maybe you’re looking for a new challenge: a role where ambition, people skills, and results are truly rewarded. Either way, this is your chance to build something exciting. **What the role involves:** As a Recruitment Consultant, you’ll be backed by a leadership team dedicated to helping you grow quickly and confidently. Every day brings variety and purpose: * Building relationships with clients and candidates across the healthcare sector * Managing live vacancies from first briefing to final offer * Interviewing, qualifying, and advising candidates through each stage of the process **What’s in it for you:** * **Career Progression** \- Rise fast through a clear, merit\-based pathway into senior and leadership positions. * **Continuous Development** \- Access expert mentorship, hands\-on training, and specialist knowledge to accelerate your career. * **Recognition \& Rewards** \- Every milestone matters. Expect real recognition, added responsibility, and outstanding financial rewards. The **average total compensation for year 1 is more than €45k, and for year 2 it’s over €75k** . Of course, due to our uncapped commission structure, top performers earn much more than this from the jump. * **Leadership Academy** \- Exclusive access to our Leadership Academy for advanced training, certifications, and networking with top performers. **You’ll be a great fit if you have:** * At least 1 year of experience in a fast\-moving environment (any sector) * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Your success story could look like Rian’s:** In just **24 months** , he progressed from Recruitment Consultant **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. For more information, contact César Toscano at **\+353 1 572 1161** or email your CV to **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: Accounting Technician / PQ Management Accountant
description: **Your new company** An established semi\-state organisation at the forefront of delivering affordable housing in Ireland is expanding its Asset Management function. With a significant national development pipeline and a mission centred on creating sustainable, thriving communities, the organisation offers a dynamic environment where your work will contribute to long‑term social impact. As part of a growing team, you will collaborate across Finance, Audit, Tax, and operational business units to support the delivery of high‑quality cost‑rental housing. **Your new role** As an Accounts Technician (Part Qualified), you will report to the Revenue Manager and play a key role in management accounting, performance reporting, and financial analysis within the Cost Rental Operations team.Your responsibilities will include: * Analysing monthly financial and data reports from third‑party agents and preparing consolidated portfolio performance reports. * Assisting in the preparation of annual budgets for the cost‑rental portfolio and Asset Management department. * Monitoring and reporting on key KPIs such as occupancy, arrears, and operating expenses. * Benchmarking performance across the portfolio to identify efficiencies and opportunities for improvement. * Preparing monthly financial reports for senior management, including narrative commentary. * Managing purchase orders and invoices to ensure compliance with internal policies. * Supporting audit processes and resolving procurement and accounts payable queries. * Preparing weekly and monthly internal reports for meetings and project reviews. * Building strong working relationships with internal teams and external stakeholders. **What you'll need to succeed** * Minimum 3 years' experience in a similar accounting or finance role. * Experience working with property‑related IT systems and reporting tools. * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word. * Previous experience as an Accounts Technician or Management Accountant in the residential rental sector is a distinct advantage. * Excellent organisational skills and strong attention to detail. * Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines. * A collaborative mindset with the ability to work cross‑functionally. * A positive, solutions‑focused attitude and a commitment to confidentiality and professionalism. * Part‑qualified or actively pursuing an accounting qualification. **What you'll get in return** You will join a rapidly expanding organisation with a meaningful mission and a strong pipeline of impactful projects. This role offers: * A permanent, full‑time position based in Dublin. * Opportunities for professional development as the organisation continues to scale. * Exposure to a wide range of financial, operational, and strategic activities. * The chance to contribute to a nationally significant programme delivering long‑term social value. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. **Desired Skills and Experience** Minimum 3 years' experience in a similar accounting or finance role. Experience working with property‑related IT systems and reporting tools. Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word. Previous experience as an Accounts Technician or Management Accountant in the residential rental sector is a distinct advantage. Excellent organisational skills and strong attention to detail. Ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and meet deadlines. A collaborative mindset with the ability to work cross‑functionally. A positive, solutions‑focused attitude and a commitment to confidentiality and professionalism. Part‑qualified or actively pursuing an accounting qualification. title: Activity Facilitator Permanent
description: **Details of Service** Leopardstown Park Hospital is a registered centre for the provision of services to older people, it provides Day services and Long\-Term Care, situated in South Co. Dublin. It was established in 1917 and continues to provide interdisciplinary services on a seven\-day, twenty\-four\-hour basis **.** It is currently registered for 120 long stay beds **Salary Scale for Position €36,288 \- €45,484** Reporting Relationship : The post holder is: professionally accountable to the Assistant Director of Nursing will work closely with the Occupational Therapist Manager Purpose of the Post To provide an Activities Service to residents of LPH To support the Assistant Director of Nursing and Occupational Therapist Manager and wider OT team in the professional planning, preparation, and delivery of the Activity Timetable/Programme, with a key focus on person centred experience. To foster a collaborative approach to working with other disciplines, managers, and departments. To oversee the use and management of activities resources. The Activities Facilitator plays a key role in providing the Activities Programme Principal Duties and Responsibilities The Activities Facilitator will: Provide a high level of leadership in activities delivery. Provide safe comprehensive acivities services to residents within the scheduled programme. Provide meaningful activities suitable to the individual and group function and presentation. Recognise when adaptations in activities may be required and liaise with OT dept staff in relation to same. Work closely with the other disciplines to facilitate the smooth running of activities events and programmes in conjunction with the ADON, OT Manager, OT staff and others as appropriate. The Activities Facilitator will practice nursing according to Hospital Guidelines, policies, protocols, guidelines and current relevant legislation and regulatory standards relating to Older Persons Services. Manage own workload in accordance with the needs of the post. Participate in teams as appropriate, communicating and working in cooperation with other team members. Ensure that residents/patients and others are treated with dignity and respect. Ensure the maintenance of records in accordance with LPH standards and record on E ipiCare and elsewhere as required. Adhere to and contribute to the development and maintenance of activities standards, protocols and guidelines consistent with the highest standards of care. Manage the implementation of best practice policy and procedures. Participate in clinical audit with the QPS, as required Actively manage the Activities programme and work collaboratively with the OT Department to oversee the provision of meaningful activities across the hospital. Participate in activities for all residents groups and individuals, including those with dementias, learning difficulties, physical incapacities, etc. To undertake such duties appropriate to the post as may be assigned by the Assistant Director of Nursing and/or Occupational Therapist Manager or their deputies. Health \& Safety The Activities Facilitator will: Ensure adherence to established policies and procedures e.g., health and safety, infection control, storage and use of equipment etc. Participate in completion of incident / near miss forms (NIRF). Maintain a feedback mechanism with the ADON and OT Manager Ensure adherence to department and hospital policies and guidelines in relation to the care and safety of environment and equipment supplied/used for the fulfilment of duty. Participate in risk identification and management processes. Education and Training The Activities Facilitator will: Contribute to service development through appropriate continuous education, research initiatives, keeping up to date with activities practitioners and developments and attend staff study days as considered appropriate. **Other Requirements** Show innovation and creativity. Ability to motivate self and others. Demonstrate flexibility. Ability to proactively work with other members of the team. Solution focused approach to problem solving. The above Job Specification is not intended to be a comprehensive list of all duties involved and consequently, the post holder may be required to perform other duties as appropriate to the post which may be assigned to him/her from time to time and to contribute to the development of the post while in office. Eligibility Criteria **Qualifications And/ Or Experience** Candidates must Be educated to at least QQI level V in the Care of the Older Person. Demonstrate evidence of continuing professional development at the appropriate level. Age restrictions shall only apply to a candidate where he/she is not classified as a new entrant (within the meaning of the Public Service Superannuation Act, 2004\). A candidate who is not classified as a new entrant must be under 65 ears of a e. **Leopardstown Park Hospital is an equal opportunities employer.** **Closing date for applications is Monday 13th April 2026 @ 4\.00pm**

title: Analytics Program Assoc
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** The **Analytics Development Program** is a full\-time opportunity designed for early career professionals eager to launch their careers in data analysis and data science. This program empowers participants to contribute within UnitedHealth Group, helping shape the future of healthcare through data\-driven insights. We're developing UnitedHealth Group's next generation of analytical leaders and seeking high\-performing high\-potential recent graduates to drive the future of healthcare. Participants will be supported by experienced program leads and managers who provide guidance and mentorship, helping them translate academic experience into real\-world value and efficiency across a large healthcare system. This role will require you be in office in a hybrid capacity and reside within a commutable distance of the following office: Dublin, Ireland **Please note:** Relocation assistance will not be available for this position. **The full\-time program will provide:** * Cohort based start date \- Tuesday, July 28, 2026 * 1 year program with final placement in July 2027 * Mentorship \& buddy system * Leadership exposure * Access to Live, self\-led training and development tools sessions * After completion of the Analytics Development Program, you will transition to a final placement role within the organization. This may be in the same team or an opportunity across the wider enterprise **Our program has a primary focus on the following:** * **Data Analysis** \- Using data visualization and statistics to find and share actionable insights * **Data Science** \- Building machine learning models to solve current healthcare challenges Careers with Optum offer flexible work arrangements and individuals who live and work in the Republic of Ireland will have the opportunity to split their monthly work hours between our Dublin office and telecommuting from a home\-based office in a hybrid work model. **Primary Responsibilities:** * Aid in the development, and implementation of new processes, applications, and enhancements to existing applications * Utilize pertinent data and facts to identify and solve a range of problems * Conceive and share insights to appropriate audiences * Subject matter expertise (SME) in the continued design and development of in\-house software applications * Collaborate with fellow data scientists and subject matter experts to design and oversee the development of working prototypes of data science solutions * Extract data for various reports and analyses, including database improvement and maintenance * Assess processes for efficiency and performance; utilize findings to successfully influence change * Design and implement new database tools * Support and drive analytic efforts around machine learning and innovation * Communicate analytic results and predictive models to business partners * Manipulation and analysis of data in support of a variety of analytic solutions * Identify and critique potential solutions to system/business challenges * Contribute to various research projects pertaining to business needs * Partner with cross\-functional teams to support strategic business initiatives * Other projects as assigned **Please note:** Your responsibilities will vary based on team and role. You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Qualifications:** * Final year Bachelor's degree candidate or Bachelor's degree obtained within the past 24 months from an accredited college or university; all degree coursework must be completed prior to the employment start date and verified within 6\-8 weeks following graduation, or equivalent experience * Completion of an analytics related course (e.g., data analysis, statistics, economics, machine learning * Proven experience working with SQL, data visualization, Python or Tableau * Proven eligibility to work in Ireland without company sponsorship **Preferred Qualifications:** * Currently pursuing a degree in Information Technology, Healthcare, Project Management, Business, Finance, Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Data Analytics, or another business related field * Demonstrated intermediate proficiency with PC based software applications and automated database management systems (e.g., Excel, Access, PowerPoint) * Proven previous experience in an analytical based internship * Proven relevant healthcare business related experience * Proven data analysis experience through student clubs, personal projects or other forms of employment **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA

title: Application Support Analyst

You will collaborate with development teams, Operations, and other key business stakeholders. This demanding and technical role requires strong technical systems and application knowledge to make critical support decisions under pressure. You will also manage complex project work for new or existing support systems, monitoring systems, support tools, and the deployment of new software trading systems. **Responsibilities** * Analyse and diagnose issues raised by business users related to trade data or system configurations. * Maintain and manage the batch scheduling environment and monitoring systems. * Remotely manage and monitor server hosts in multiple colocations across Europe. * Manage and prioritise requests from internal IT support functions. * Set up and maintain tools that monitor for alerts and test new system changes. * Continuously evaluate, improve, and deploy new application versions. * Serve as a contact point and collaborate with other infrastructure and software teams to ensure scalability, and stability. * Manage project work, including hardware refreshes, environment optimisation, software configurations, change management, and release management. **What We're Looking For** * + Excellent IT analytical and troubleshooting skills, with extensive experience tracing problems through log file analysis and understanding application/systems logic. + Experience with or supporting a batch scheduling system is an advantage. + Experience with application monitoring and analytical tools such as Splunk and Check\_mk. + SQL, DevOps and automation experience. + Experience in finance is a significant plus. + A B.S. in a technical discipline or equivalent experience. Knowledge of or a desire to develop networked application systems, including exposure to database environments and Windows (2012, 2019\). **Personal Skills** * Initiative: Proven record of seeking continuous improvement and implementing best practices in a technology environment. * Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines. * Self\-motivated and creative. * Strong communication skills (verbal and written): Ability to convey technically complex subjects clearly and concisely. * Attention to detail: Thorough in work carried out. * Interpersonal skills: Friendly and approachable. * Teamwork: Ability to work collaboratively to meet deadlines. * Excellent time management, organisation, and prioritisation skills. * Flexibility: Desire to learn and improve skills and knowledge. Desire to drive business impact. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to* *recruiting@sig.com* *. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*

title: Applied AI Engineer
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions — collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love. We’re looking for an Applied AI Lead who knows how to take responsibility over the roadmap of their product, including day\-to\-day operations, project execution, and addressing risks. From conception to launch and beyond, you’ll collaborate cross\-functionally and communicate with senior management to be the go\-to person for your product. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Owning the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for AI\-powered solutions, leveraging LLMs and other machine learning technologies * Collaborating closely with Data Scientists and Software Engineers to deliver impactful solutions * Leading cross\-functional teams to build and deploy NLP and AI solutions, such as AI assistants and AI automation platforms * Setting your team's goals, success metrics, and roadmap to align with company goals * Delivering measurable results for sophisticated products under ambiguous situations in a fast\-paced environment **What You'll Need** * Experience developing and launching AI products/features from scratch * High analytical and technical acumen, along with solid business sense and problem\-solving skills * Experience in a data science or machine learning role * Experience owning machine learning projects, from design to implementation and iteration * Great Python, SQL, or other programming language skills * Hands\-on experience with deep learning libs (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, etc.) * Knowledge of version control, model deployment, and developer environments * Evidence of impressive mathematical and statistical knowledge * An entrepreneurial drive to never settle Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Expertise with Amazon Web Services (AWS), particularly: * AWS Lambda * API Gateway * DynamoDB * Programming skills and a background in Python * Gen AI and Intelligent systems that include LLM, RAG and Agentic AI * Hands on experience with Gen AI systems would be an advantage * Excellent understanding of software development best practices, design patterns, and SDLC methodologies. **Desirable** *:* * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. * Experience with CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and cloud ecosystems. * Understanding of data governance, security architecture, and compliance in cloud environments. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities

title: Associate - Health & Prosecutions
description: **Practice Area:** Health \& Prosecutions **Position(s):** Associate **PQE:** NQ \- 4 **Position Type:** Permanent **Firm:** Mason Hayes \& Curran LLP is a leading Irish law firm based in Dublin with offices in San Francisco, New York and London. We are a business law firm with extensive knowledge and experience in the following sectors: Energy; Technology; Financial Services, Built Environment and Healthcare \& Life Sciences. **The Role** We are seeking a motivated associate to join our Health \& Prosecutions team for an exciting and rewarding role. Our unique team of lawyers do the majority of our own advocacy in the courts, giving us an unrivalled perspective when it comes to initial advice and case preparation. We advise on healthcare law, child protection law and regulatory prosecutions. We also develop tailored legal training programmes, workshops and webinars for professionals. We act on behalf of various statutory authorities including TUSLA Child and Family Agency, the Health Service Executive, private hospitals and local authorities. Interest in developing advocacy skills is important and the role will require travel outside of Dublin. **Candidate Requirements** * Qualified as a solicitor in Ireland, England or Wales. * Experience in a related practice area. * A team player who can work with different teams and lawyers across the firm. * Ability to deliver legal training where required. * Keen to progress their career in an international firm. The ideal candidate will have experience with: * Litigation. * Child protection work. * Healthcare in the field of mental health, wards of court and disability. * Prosecutions. * Court advocacy preferred but not essential. **Why MHC?** * Excellent remuneration and benefits package; pension, healthcare \& bonus. * Enviable client base and strong career progression opportunities. * Friendly and informal working atmosphere. * Flexible/hybrid working arrangements. * Friendly, open and informal working atmosphere. * Focus on employee involvement, wellbeing and success. * Social events, clubs and initiatives (Green Team, Working on Wellness, subsidised fitness classes, MHC RISE, Diversity, Sports \& Social and Art Committee). * MHC knowledge and education programme to facilitate major development as a lawyer. **Learn more about Mason, Hayes \& Curran** * Learn more about a Career at Mason, Hayes \& Curran **HERE** * See more about Mason, Hayes \& Curran and our teams on our YouTube **HERE** * Visit our LinkedIn **HERE** So, if you like challenge, value autonomy, work well within a team, enjoy partnering with senior business stakeholders and love working with top notch clients, then apply to join us @MASON HAYES \& CURRAN. We invite you to grow your career with us!

title: Associate - Litigation and Regulation  (min 2 years PQE)
description: The Litigation \& Regulation practice at Byrne Wallace Shields LLP provides a comprehensive service on all aspects of corporate insolvency, personal insolvency and corporate restructuring. Our team advises leading insolvency practitioners, financial institutions, alternative lenders, investors, unsecured creditors, debtors, directors and shareholders on both contentious and non\-contentious insolvency and restructuring issues. We are looking for agile and adaptable candidates that have both contentious and advisory experience who can handle work a varied and/or complex workload. Candidates should also have a strong academic track record. If you have a minimum of 2 years PQE from a top or mid\-tier law firm, or you have an interesting in\-house role, or if you’re returning to Ireland then we’d love to hear from you. Key Responsibilities include; * Advising a varied client base which includes insolvency practitioners (as appointed office holders), banks and other financial institutions, corporates, creditors, directors and shareholders on both contentious and non\-contentious corporate recovery and insolvency matters. * Advising and acting for clients in respect of the following – * Advising secured creditors in relation to enforcement options * Trading and Fixed Asset Receiverships * All aspects of Insolvent and solvent liquidations * Examinerships and Part Schemes * Advising on directors’ duties * Cross border insolvency (both corporate and individual) * Advising in commercial litigation matters including managing litigation caseload in all courts with an emphasis on the superior courts to include drafting pleadings, liaising with counsel (where applicable) and management of case files to conclusion. * Advising and drafting in regulatory matters for clients * Advising on data protection and cybersecurity disputes * Advising on AI governance, compliance and emerging technology regulation * Interpreting, analysing and advising clients on relevant case law and legislation * Management of the client relationship * Working with senior department members on a variety of matters * Advising clients on ADR options, and participating in mediations and arbitrations * Advising clients on how to resolve disputes effectively For more information on Byrne Wallace Shields LLP, see www.byrnewallaceshields.com

title: Associate QC
description: **Overview:** Responsible for planning, executing and documenting the qualification, calibration and routine maintenance of laboratory systems for cGMP Quality Control laboratory facilities. Responsible for ensuring that all maintenance \& calibration activities carried out by Calibration Technicians are tracked \& managed through the Computerised Maintenance Management System (CMMS) Maximo. Candidate should have specific hands\-on laboratory equipment lifecycle and validation experience. **Key Responsibilities include:** \* Planning and conducting routine calibration, re qualification and maintenance of laboratory equipment ensuring calibration and maintenance schedules are adhered to as per CMMS Maximo. Reviewing \& filing of QC calibration \& maintenance documentation. \* Planning, executing and documenting the qualification of new laboratory equipment in a cGMP regulated environment, including the writing of equipment validation protocols and associated summary reports. \* Conduct periodic reviews of instrument validation as part of validation life cycle. \* Alerting Quality Control Management in the event of equipment failing to meet calibration or qualification requirements and conducting impact assessments/investigations as required. \* Scheduling and coordination of equipment repairs and maintenance with vendors/contractors, while minimizing level of down\-time for lab equipment, and disruption to laboratory activities. \* Participation in technical project teams to act as a subject matter expert on instrument validation regulations and procedures. \* Where applicable, owns and project manages change controls and adherence to Change Control metrics \* Supporting and managing the addition of project components to CMMA Maximo \* Act as a key contributor and sponsor to Data Integrity Assessments for Lab systems \* Any other tasks/projects assigned as per manger's request **Knowledge and Experience:** \* Bachelor's degree in a Science related field is required. \* Minimum of 3 years of laboratory experience in a cGMP laboratory environment including exposure to analytical instrument validation and data integrity assessments. \* Detailed knowledge of the methodology, and instrumentation and analytical techniques used for biopharmaceutical testing. \* Understanding of current regulatory requirements for cGMP laboratory equipment validation and electronic record integrity. \* Demonstrated success in managing an equipment qualification or maintenance program advantageous. The successful candidates will be involved with routine activities and may be requested to support project work. For the most part project work consists of the introduction and qualification of analytical equipment for use in routine QC analysis and the candidate should have specific hands\-on laboratory equipment lifecycle and validation

title: Associate Software Engineer
description: **Job Overview** At iCIMS, we’re on a mission to redefine how people connect with opportunity through intelligent, human\-centered technology. Our Dublin AI Hub is the strategic center for innovation, where we’re building the next generation of our Talent Cloud platform—powered by Natural Language Processing (NLP), Large Language Models (LLMs), and Machine Learning. As an Associate Full Stack Engineer, you'll develop REST API services and single\-page applications primarily using TypeScript, Node.js, and React, whilst learning to integrate AI and NLP capabilities into production systems. You'll collaborate with data scientists, product managers, and engineers in a culture that values innovation, ownership, and continuous learning. This is a hybrid position based at Windmill Lane, Dublin 2, our strategic hub for AI development in Ireland. **About Us** When you join iCIMS, you join the team helping global companies transform business and the world through the power of talent. Our customers do amazing things: design rocket ships, create vaccines, deliver consumer goods globally, overnight, with a smile. As the Talent Cloud company, we empower these organizations to attract, engage, hire, and advance the right talent. We’re passionate about helping companies build a diverse, winning workforce and about building our home team. We're dedicated to fostering an inclusive, purpose\-driven, and innovative work environment where everyone belongs. **Responsibilities** * Develop, test, and maintain scalable user interfaces using React and TypeScript, and REST API services using Node.js * Integrate AI, large language models, natural language processing and agentic capabilities into production services * Ensure components are reliable, secure, and performant in cloud environments (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) * Collaborate with product managers, engineers and data scientists across the stack to deliver small features * Write comprehensive automated tests and participate in code reviews to maintain code quality * Learn and apply modern engineering practices including CI/CD, agile methodologies, and cloud deployment * Grow your technical skills with support from mentors and training resources **Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent professional experience) * Proficiency in at least one of: JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, or React * Understanding of modern web development, including REST APIs and single\-page application architectures * Familiarity with software engineering practices (e.g., version control, testing, code reviews) * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with intellectual curiosity * Strong communication and collaboration skills across both technical and non\-technical teams **EEO Statement** iCIMS is a place where everyone belongs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at iCIMS. We are proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, veteran status, genetic information, disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. If you would like to request an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at careers@icims.com. **Compensation And Benefits** iCIMS offers a comprehensive benefits package: medical, vision, and dental, life insurance, pensions, tuition reimbursement, lifestyle spending accounts, and generous paid time off plans (vacation, sick, parental leave). Learn more here: https://careers.icims.com/benefits

title: Basic Grade Social Worker - CDNT
description: Dublin, IrelandPart\-time, Fixed Term Contract€ 50,621 \- 73,080 Fluent EnglishClosing date: May 26, 2026 11:00 AM **Who We Are** -------------- St. Michael’s House provides a comprehensive range of services and supports to men, women, and children with disabilities and their families in 170 locations in the greater Dublin Area. It supports 1,953 people and this has an impact on thousands of family members. St. Michael’s House is a company funded by the Health Service Executive (HSE), TUSLA and the Department of Education and Skills. (Figures from 1st May 2021\). St. Michael’s House supports include; Residential supports and Independent **Description** --------------- **Professionally Qualified Basic Grade Social Work** **Part Time, Fixed Term 12 month Contract** **Coolock Children’s Disability Network Team** This is an exciting opportunity for a basic grade Social Worker to support children and their families, presenting with complex needs arising from a combination of intellectual, physical, and/or sensory disabilities. The Social Worker is a valuable member of the Children’s Disability Network Team (CDNT), will work as part of a multi\-disciplinary team. The structure with in each CDNT comprises of a Social Work Team Leader, Senior Practitioner and Basic Grade posts. There will be opportunities for training and regular clinical supervision is provided. This temporary post is assigned to the Coolock Children’s Disability Network Team. Staff will see children in the Coolock CDNT offices in Coolock Primary Care Centre, Cromcastle Road. Children will also be seen at home and in preschool/school. **The successful candidate must have the following:** **Essential criteria:** Degree or Masters in Social Work or equivalent National Qualification in Social Work (NQSW) Be fully registered with CORU Be committed to the delivery of high quality, person\-centred, community\-based services Have excellent communication, time management skills and the ability to work in a self directed manner. Full clean drivers license and access to a car **Desirable criteria:** Experience working in children’s disability services. Have experience working as part of a multidisciplinary team. **A panel will be created for future employment opportunities, across the four St Michael’s House Children’s Disability Network Teams. Pending graduates may apply.** **Benefits of working in St Michael's House** HSE Pay Scale (incremental\*) Premium Payments (Frontline staff) Generous Annual Leave Sick Pay Scheme Paid Maternity Leave Pension Career Progression Cycle to Work Scheme Employee Assistance Programme Training / CPD **To Apply** A comprehensive CV, detailing education, skills, career history, experience. A short cover letter/personal statement (i.e., no more than 2 pages) outlining why you wish to be considered for the post and where you believe your skills, experience and values meet the requirements of this role with St. Michael’s House. **Only candidates shortlisted for interview will be contacted.** **Salary scale :** €50,621 \- €73,080 LSI Should you have no prior public sector experience you will be placed on point 1 of the pay\-scale above **Informal enquires:** Niamh Giltinan: Coolock Children’s Disability Network Manager, niamh.giltinan@smh.ie Catherine Rafter: Principal Social Worker, Catherine.rafter@smh.ie **Closing Date:** 26th May 2026, 5:00pm We endeavour to give as much notice as possible for the interview dates etc. Candidates should make themselves available on the date(s) All interviews will be held in person **Candidates should note that canvassing will disqualify them.** **St. Michael’s House is an equal opportunities employer.**

title: Biology Researcher (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Biology Researcher (Masters/PhDs) \- AI Training** **About The Role** What if your years of biology expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex problems in genetics, biotechnology, and bioinformatics? We're looking for advanced biology experts to help build and train cutting\-edge AI models — ensuring they think with the depth, rigor, and precision that real science demands. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role designed for Masters and PhD\-level biologists who want to make a meaningful impact beyond the lab. No prior AI experience required — your scientific expertise is exactly what we need. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design, solve, and critically evaluate advanced biology problems with real\-world scientific relevance * Apply your expertise in genetics, biotechnology, bioinformatics, or related disciplines to create complex, nuanced problem sets * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to enhance AI model reasoning and accuracy * Assess the scientific rigor, clarity, and depth of AI\-generated biological content * Ensure outputs meet the standards expected of graduate\-level research and scholarship **Who You Are** * Master's or PhD in Biology or a closely related field — Genetics, Molecular Biology, Bioinformatics, Biotechnology, or equivalent * Strong working knowledge of genetics, biotechnology, or bioinformatics * Comfortable coding in Python or R for research or data analysis tasks * Exceptional written communicator — able to explain complex science with precision and clarity * Detail\-oriented, methodical, and committed to scientific accuracy * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently and asynchronously **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, data quality review, or AI evaluation workflows * Background in academic publishing, peer review, or scientific writing * Familiarity with computational biology tools or bioinformatics pipelines * Experience designing or evaluating research problems in an academic or industry setting **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside the world's leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — structure your hours around your life, not the other way around * Freelance autonomy with meaningful, intellectually stimulating work * Put your advanced degree to work in a fast\-growing, high\-impact field * Contribute to AI systems that will shape how science is understood and communicated at scale * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Boat Shop Summer Junior Warehouse Helper – Marine Parts Distributor
description: Summer Junior Warehouse Helper – Marine Parts Distributor We are looking for a reliable and hardworking Summer Junior Warehouse Helper to join our busy marine parts distribution company for the summer season. Main Duties: * Picking and packing customer orders * Receiving and checking deliveries * Keeping the warehouse clean and organised * Assisting with stock control and shelving products * Helping with loading and unloading deliveries * Supporting the warehouse team with daily tasks during our busy summer period Requirements: * Good attitude and willingness to learn * Reliable and punctual * Able to work as part of a team * Basic computer skills an advantage * Interest in boats or marine products helpful but not essential This is a temporary summer position and would suit a student or someone looking for seasonal work experience in a growing marine business. Pay: €12\.00\-€14\.15 per hour Education: * Leaving Certificate (preferred) Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Brand Writer
description: ****About Remote**** Remote is solving modern organizations’ biggest challenge – navigating global employment compliantly with ease. We make it possible for businesses of all sizes to recruit, pay, and manage international teams. With our core values at heart and future focused work culture, our team works tirelessly on ambitious problems, asynchronously, around the world. You can find Remoters working from 6 different continents (Antarctica left to go!) and all of our positions are fully remote. With Innovation as one of the core values, we have built Automation and AI capabilities into the requirements for every role. We encourage every member of the Remote team to bring their talents, experiences and culture to the table to help us build the best\-in\-class HR platform. If you are energetic, curious, motivated and ambitious, be part of our world. Apply now and define the future of work! ****About Remote**** Remote is solving the biggest challenge in modern business: building and scaling global teams. We enable companies to employ anyone, anywhere compliantly, while simplifying payroll, benefits, taxes, and HR. Our mission is to create opportunity everywhere — and storytelling plays a critical role in making that real. ****What You’ll Do**** **Define and evolve Remote’s brand voice** Shape a distinctive, consistent voice that reflects Remote’s clarity, confidence, and global perspective. Establish tone and editorial standards that scale across campaigns and our website. **Own creative campaign storytelling end\-to\-end** Lead messaging and writing for creative campaigns — from big ideas and narratives to execution across channels. Develop work that is memorable, differentiated, and deeply aligned with our audience. **Elevate the Remote website** Write and optimize high\-impact website content that clearly communicates our value, simplifies complex topics, and drives conversion. Continuously refine messaging based on performance and audience insight. **Create scalable content systems** Develop repeatable formats, messaging frameworks, and modular content that can be reused and adapted across campaigns and web experiences. **Turn attention into action** Craft content that doesn’t just inform — it moves people. Help transform visitors into customers, and customers into advocates through compelling, conversion\-oriented storytelling. **Experiment and push the brand forward** Explore new formats, channels, and creative approaches. Bring fresh, modern storytelling techniques into Remote’s brand ecosystem. **Scale with AI (thoughtfully)** Leverage AI to increase speed, consistency, and creative ambition — while maintaining a strong editorial point of view and human quality bar. **Collaborate across Remote** Partner with marketing, product, design, and leadership to bring sharp, differentiated perspectives to life. Translate complex global employment topics into clear, engaging narratives. **Raise the bar for brand writing** Set a high standard for craft, clarity, and creativity. Continuously improve how we tell Remote’s story across every touchpoint. ****What You Bring**** * Proven experience writing high\-quality brand and marketing content across formats — especially creative campaigns and web * Exceptional writing and editing skills, with a strong sense of voice, tone, and narrative structure * Strong editorial judgment, including knowing what not to create * Experience translating complex topics into clear, engaging, and accessible content * Deep understanding of modern content patterns (clarity, structure, flow, and conversion principles) * A clear perspective on how AI can enhance content creation * Track record of driving measurable impact through content (brand, engagement, and conversion) * Ability to collaborate cross\-functionally and influence stakeholders * Experience in HR tech, SaaS, or fintech — or strong curiosity about the future of work and global employment **Why This Role Matters** This role sits at the heart of how Remote shows up in the world. You’ll help define how we tell our story — making complex global employment feel simple, human, and compelling. Your work will shape first impressions, drive growth, and build a brand that companies trust as they expand globally. **Application process** * Interview with recruiter * Interview with future manager * Interview with team members * Bar Raiser Interview * Prior employment verification check Remote's Total Rewards philosophy is to ensure fair, unbiased compensation and fair equity pay along with competitive benefits in all locations in which we operate. We do not agree to or encourage cheap\-labor practices and therefore we ensure to pay above in\-location rates. We hope to inspire other companies to support global talent\-hiring and bring local wealth to developing countries. At first glance our salary bands seem quite wide \- here is some context. At Remote we have international operations and a globally distributed workforce. We use geo ranges to consider geographic pay differentials as part of our global compensation strategy to remain competitive in various markets while we hiring globally. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location, and our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay for the successful candidate in this role is dependent upon many factors such as location, transferable or job\-related skills, work experience, relevant training, business needs, and market demands. The base salary range may be subject to change. *At Remote, we foster internal mobility as a key element of our culture of employee growth and development, supported by a compensation philosophy that guarantees pay equity and fairness. Therefore, all compensation changes associated with an internal move will be reviewed by the Total Rewards \& People Enablement team on a case by case basis.* The annual salary range for this full\-time position is $90,000—$120,000 USD ****Benefits**** Our full benefits \& perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters: * work from anywhere * flexible paid time off * flexible working hours (we are async) * 16 weeks paid parental leave * mental health support services * stock options * learning budget * home office budget \& IT equipment * budget for local in\-person social events or co\-working spaces ****How You’ll Plan Your Day (and Life)**** We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async. You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your **life\-work balance** is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs. If that sounds like something you want, apply now! ****How To Apply**** * Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format. * We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote. * If you don’t have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead. Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under\-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask \& encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter. We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it’s important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to. At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here. *Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.*

Continuously improve how we tell Remote's story across every touchpoint. **What You Bring** ------------------ * Proven experience writing high\-quality brand and marketing content across formats — especially creative campaigns and web * Exceptional writing and editing skills, with a strong sense of voice, tone, and narrative structure * Strong editorial judgment, including knowing what *not* to create * Experience translating complex topics into clear, engaging, and accessible content * Deep understanding of modern content patterns (clarity, structure, flow, and conversion principles) * A clear perspective on how AI can enhance content creation * Track record of driving measurable impact through content (brand, engagement, and conversion) * Ability to collaborate cross\-functionally and influence stakeholders * Experience in HR tech, SaaS, or fintech — or strong curiosity about the future of work and global employment **Why This Role Matters** ------------------------- This role sits at the heart of how Remote shows up in the world. You'll help define how we tell our story — making complex global employment feel simple, human, and compelling. Your work will shape first impressions, drive growth, and build a brand that companies trust as they expand globally. **Application process** ----------------------- * Interview with recruiter * Interview with future manager * Interview with team members * Bar Raiser Interview * Prior employment verification check **Benefits** ------------ Our full benefits \& perks are explained in our handbook at remote.com/r/benefits. As a global company, each country works differently, but some benefits/perks are for all Remoters: * work from anywhere * flexible paid time off * flexible working hours (we are async) * 16 weeks paid parental leave * mental health support services * stock options * learning budget * home office budget \& IT equipment * budget for local in\-person social events or co\-working spaces **How you'll plan your day (and life)** --------------------------------------- We work async at Remote which means you can plan your schedule around your life (and not around meetings). Read more at remote.com/async. You will be empowered to take ownership and be proactive. When in doubt you will default to action instead of waiting. Your **life\-work balance** is important and you will be encouraged to put yourself and your family first, and fit work around your needs. If that sounds like something you want, apply now! **How to apply** ---------------- * Please fill out the form below and upload your CV with a PDF format. * **We kindly ask you to submit your application and CV in English, as this is the standardised language we use here at Remote.** * If you don't have an up to date CV but you are still interested in talking to us, please feel free to add a copy of your LinkedIn profile instead. Not only do we encourage folks from all ethnic groups, genders, sexuality, age, abilities, disability status and any other under\-represented group to apply, but we prioritize a sense of belonging. We have 4 ERGs (Women, Disability, Queer, Minorities in Tech) who meet regularly with the People team. During your interviews and beyond, we ask \& encourage anybody who needs an accommodation to request one from their recruiter. We will ask you to voluntarily tell us your pronouns at interview stage, and you will have the option to answer our anonymous demographic questionnaire when you apply below. As an equal employment opportunity employer it's important to us that our workforce reflects people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences and this data will help us to stay accountable. We thank you for providing this data, if you chose to. At Remote, we embrace AI as a valuable tool while prioritizing human creativity and authenticity. We look forward to meeting candidates who balance innovation with genuine expertise and experience. To learn more about Remote's AI guidelines check see here. *Please note we accept applications on an ongoing basis.*

title: Business Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an experienced and **Senior Business Analyst** to join their team on a daily rate contract Hybrid position based in Dublin with 2 days per week on site. **Job Responsibilities** * Gather, define and validate business requirements from multiple stakeholders for development and implementation of key products, tools and analytics * Lead and contribute towards project delivery and success * Analyse and understand business workflow and provide solutions for product enhancements and new initiatives and provide ongoing support for existing products * Collaborate with technology groups, business colleagues and other teams * Focus on the delivery of high quality, user\-centric products, and features * Work closely with software developers and other colleagues to ensure that they understand the requirements and the features * Support and co\-ordinate testing activities across all phases of projects including unit testing, integration testing, user acceptance testing and downstream testing * Collect and track feedback from the end users for future application and analytics design and provide recommendations in developing business solutions for business stakeholders **Experience Required** * Demonstrable experience in business analysis or similar role * Previous experience within a similar role with evidence of successful delivery of product and business change outcomes * Self\-starter with the ability to learn and work independently and as part of a team * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with knowledge of Agile processes and principles * Experience of taking ownership of stakeholder deliverables * Experience in digital product or platform development * Experience working with multi\-functional teams that include business partners, domain experts, analysts, UX designers, IT architects * Excellent communication skills with very strong interpersonal skills * Stakeholder Engagement Experience is a must * Experience with customer journe y design and customer experience implementations would be a bonus but not essential * Demonstrable ability to complete projects according to outlined scope, budget, and timeline **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities.

title: Business Developer Project Intern (TikTok Shop) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)
description: **Responsibilities** We are looking for an ambitious Intern to support TikTok Commerce in Ireland. The role is part of the Account Management team which is pivotal to onboarding and ensuring the success of key partners on our platform, curating an engaging product selection for TikTok users. Together, we'll redefine how partners engage with customers through content and social commerce, creating a unique and innovative commerce ecosystem. As a project intern, you will have the opportunity to engage in impactful short\-term projects that provide you with a glimpse of professional real\-world experience. You will gain practical skills through on\-the\-job learning in a fast\-paced work environment and develop a deeper understanding of your career interests. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis \- we encourage you to apply early. **Responsibilities** * Assist in lead mining, qualifying, segmenting and other lead management works based on a data\-driven approach. * Work with the team to build showcases and case studies, including targeting, performance tracking and analysis. * Operate and Incubate TikTok Commerce via different channels such as external communities, emails, phone calls, online/offline events, helping with daily operation, related data consolidation and analysis to boost their growth. * Assist in market research and produce insights to feed the team targets and business strategies. * General administrative support to the operation team. * Other miscellaneous/administrative responsibilities as required by management. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications * Currently enrolled in a bachelor's or master's degree, graduating in 2026 * Strong written and verbal communication skills in English * Demonstrated learning ability, pro\-active and resilient, able to cope well under pressure, and pay attention to detail * A team player with excellent organizational \& planning skills * The ability to measure performance based on data analysis * Sound judgment and flexibility in balancing requirements, tight deadlines, and keeping people and projects moving on schedule Preferred Qualifications * Demonstrated computer skills and familiarity with MS office applications. Data processing using SQL or tableau is a plus. * Familiar with the E\-commerce market and social media trends Job Information **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. Diversity \& Inclusion TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Business Internship
description: Optum is a global organization that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by diversity and inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health equity on a global scale. Join us to start **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.** The business summer internship is targeted at students working towards their graduate degree in a business or science or related discipline. Other degrees will be considered when accompanied by an aptitude or interest in business practices. The role of the business intern will be to sit within one of our business teams and learn and participate in the analysis, evaluation and implementation of business decisions. **To that end, the candidate may have experience in any of the following:** * Analytical and information gathering skills * Ability to evaluate and prioritize extensive detailed data * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, conveying complex or technical information in a manner that others can understand * Ability to understand and interpret complex information * Experience successfully managing multiple requests, assessing priorities and achieving solutions under deadlines The candidate will be expected to have a basic, structured and standard approach to work. Be capable of performing routine or structured work and be able to respond to routine or standard requests. They will be expected to depend on fellow team members for instruction, guidance or direction. In return they will gain experience of business processes in a complex environment, how to prepare and present complex information in a fashion that is understandable and actionable. They will experience the value of working as a team member and gain insights into team dynamics. You will be rewarded and recognized for your performance in an environment that will challenge you and give you clear direction on what it takes to succeed in your role, as well as providing development for other roles you may be interested in. **Required Skills \& Qualifications:** * Currently working towards a graduate degree in business or science related disciplines * Demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Office, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint * Demonstrated ability to collect, measure, organize, and analyze data **Soft Skills:** * Proven ability to learn new technologies quickly * Proven ability to be professional, service oriented, proactive, and flexible * Demonstrated interpersonal and team skills * Demonstrated excellent verbal and written communication, with the ability to clearly explain complex or technical information **Please note you must currently be eligible to work and remain indefinitely without any restrictions in the country to which you are making an application. Proof will be required to support your application.** *At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live healthier lives and make the health system work better for everyone. We believe everyone\-of every race, gender, sexuality, age, location and income\-deserves the opportunity to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are still far too many barriers to good health which are disproportionately experienced by people of color, historically marginalized groups and those with lower incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes \- an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.* *Diversity creates a healthier atmosphere: Optum is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, disability, religion, age, race, and membership of the Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by law. Optum is a drug\-free workplace. © 2026 Optum Services (Ireland) Limited. All rights reserved.* \#BBMEMEA
